summary:
The distolingual extension of the mandibular impression is primarily constrained due to the activity of the inferior belly of the lateral pterygoid muscle which stabilizes the lower jaw preventing overextension necessary for precise fitting of full dentures.
```

The tool call was successful with retrieving specific information about how different parts of the lateral pterygoid play crucial roles in controlling movements like those involved when extending downward at an angle from where one would normally bite down while making a mold or cast needed for creating custom teeth replacements such as false bottom rows known medically as 'dentures'. This knowledge highlights why understanding muscular dynamics around our jaws matters greatly especially concerning procedures requiring meticulous attention towards anatomical details ensuring
```
